All right recruiting 101 which is how to get the best candidates you really want your business who are the key players in your area Probably already have jobs. Okay, and so you need to find them and you need to attract them to your business Okay, so the first part of attracting someone is building the culture because people hear about culture. I can tell you right now that 7 out of 9 people starting next Monday with us have all been referred or found us because they've heard about how great our Culture was okay referred by a client or by a client's trainer referred by somebody who already works for us So what you must understand is that if you truly over deliver to your team, then they will find your business Okay, it'll be much easier to attract them that being said the best people already have jobs or Find them within seven days of needing them. Okay So what I did want to provide you with is a little bit about how HR teams in bigger boxes do it. Okay, so One is they always plaster we are hiring signs on the doors of the gym on social media platforms Okay, so not only are they posting on all the platforms like indeed Craigslist LinkedIn all that okay But they put we are hiring on the signs of their gyms on social media and on other platforms, right? to Here's a creative idea. I Heard this from an HR manager of a 24. She bought a clothing mannequin from Amazon Put the outfit that the personal trainers would wear on it and then made a cute little display saying like this could be you right with information to talk to The manager on how to apply right so you could put that in your gym You could also put it outside whatever it may be like it's not the fact of letting people know that you're hiring It's of doing it in a creative way that intrigues them to be like what kind of business Advertises like that. What kind attracts people like that? I want to be an internal referral bonus program Okay, example would be for every person you refer that we hire you get a $500 bonus at their 90 day Okay, we actually do something similar to this and it really helped because it Incentive you are lining up incentives with what they already want to do and so it's that little push over the edge Like they already want to refer people to work for you because they think you're great And now that incentive is pushing them over the yet. So it's aligning your values with what you both want, right? Some examples for personal trainers. Okay one promote from your community. Who are your best members? Right and then two would be maybe you want to create a program where you sponsor people's PT Certifications if you think that they would be a fit, but they're not qualified yet, right? So you invest in their learning and for a lot of small businesses you're not going to invest in them Okay, what is the relationship between employees and the employer? It's a mutual exchange of trust and value So you have to give it first you have to give them value right give them value as a person Okay, and lastly something that a lot of people do when they're recruiting for personal trainers They actually wait outside of the PT certification centers to pass out information to brand new trainers or try and get them So I want to share that with you guys because that is how I know the best of the best do it It doesn't sound sexy because it's not it's just that they do it and you might not be so I wanted to give you that to have New Arsenal
